sutta: the buddha said monks when a good man is born into a family it is for the good welfare and happiness of many folk it is for the good welfare and happiness of his parents of his wife and children of his slaves workforce and men of his friends and companions it is for the good wealth and happiness of recluses and brahmanas monks just as good rain good rains bring to perfection all crops for the good welfare and happiness of many folk even so a good man is born into a family for the good welfare and happiness of many folk that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here buddha saying a good person can benefit many many persons eyes those around him and um the effect can be quite widespread depending on that person because a good man has right view and he does beneficial things beneficial good karma and because of that many people are affected but an evil man would have a wrong view and he does unwholesome kama harmful karma that would harm many people and sometimes when a child is born into a family immediately when the mother conceives the whole family is affected by this child sometimes this child brings good karma it is has been seen on many occasions sometimes immediately the father's business of fortune changes for the better so sometimes children are born can affect the whole house even from young and um concerning this benefiting other people there's 1 suita and uh we we we heard before on gudranikaya 4.61 where the buddha talked about how wealth could be used in a rightful way in a beneficial way and to benefit many people we heard about that you can use wealth to benefit yourself benefit your family benefit your relatives friends etc and even to do meritoriousness like giving to recluses and monks food the nexus is

it is for the good wealth and happiness of recluses and brahmanas

even so a good man is born into a family for the good welfare and happiness of many folk
